name: Barranc de l'Encantà Trail
name_original: La Ruta del Barranc de l'Encantá — Circular desde Planes
description: Hike the Barranc de l'Encantà Trail, a moderate 6.5-mile route with panoramic views, natural pools, and the Gorg del Salt waterfall.
region_intro: Hiking around Planes, located in Alicante, Spain, offers diverse terrain characterized by rugged mountains, lush valleys, and unique aquatic features. The region is part of a mountainous province with numerous ravines and over 50 peaks exceeding a thousand meters. This landscape provides a variety of hiking opportunities, from trails featuring natural pools and waterfalls to routes through mountain ranges and deep gorges. Best hiking trails around Planes The most popular hiking route is Barranc de l'Encantà Trail, a 6.5 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 16 minutes to complete. This route features panoramic views, natural pools, and the picturesque Gorg del Salt waterfall. Another top favourite among local hikers is Frencantada Viewpoint – Gorg del Salt de Planes loop from Planes, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.9 km) path. This loop offers scenic beauty and aquatic attractions around the prominent Gorg del Salt waterfall. Local hikers also love the Gorg del Salt de Planes – Frencantada Viewpoint loop from Toll del Teular, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the Gorg del Salt waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Hiking around Planes is defined by rugged mountains, lush valleys, and prominent waterfalls. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The routes in Planes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Planes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Planes?
A: Planes offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 170 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Planes?
A: Yes, Planes has several options for less experienced hikers and families. While many routes are moderate, there are 15 easy trails to explore. These often feature gentle terrain and beautiful scenery, such as parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5300548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg del Salt de Planes – Frencantada Viewpoint loop from Toll del Teular&lt;/a&gt;, which is shorter and less demanding.

Q: Which hikes in Planes feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Planes is renowned for its aquatic features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870062760/barranc-de-l-encanta-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de l'Encantà Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, offering panoramic views, natural pools, and the picturesque Gorg del Salt waterfall. Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31475007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frencantada Viewpoint – Gorg del Salt de Planes loop from Planes&lt;/a&gt;, which also highlights the prominent Gorg del Salt waterfall. Further afield, the Fonts d'Algar (Las Fuentes del Algar) is a protected natural area known for its stunning waterfalls and natural pools.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Planes?
A: Many of the most popular routes around Planes are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31475007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frencantada Viewpoint – Gorg del Salt de Planes loop from Planes&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5300548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg del Salt de Planes – Frencantada Viewpoint loop from Toll del Teular&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra de Mariola also offers circular trails like the one through the Cint Ravine.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking near Planes?
A: The region around Planes is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can explore various castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1076492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Benissili&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4926330" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perputxent Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2885240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penya Foradà&lt;/a&gt; and impressive summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1390751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montcabrer Summit&lt;/a&gt; offer breathtaking views. The area also features unique geological formations like the Mascarat Canyon and the Cova dels Arcs.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Planes?
A: The Planes area, being in Alicante, Spain, generally offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are ideal, with milder temperatures perfect for exploring. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, especially if you plan to visit areas with natural pools like Barranc de l'Encantà for a refreshing dip. Winters are generally mild, making it possible to hike year-round.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Planes?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Planes and its surroundings offer demanding routes. The region boasts over 50 peaks exceeding a thousand meters. For instance, the ascent of Puig Campana is an emblematic and challenging hike with over 1,000 meters of elevation gain. Another physically demanding route is the Barranc de l'Infern (Hell's Ravine), known as the 'Route of the 6,000 Steps,' which offers a unique environment of sculpted stone and rock formations.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Planes?
A: The hiking trails in Planes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 700 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, the stunning natural beauty, and the refreshing aquatic features like waterfalls and natural pools. The well-maintained paths and varied options for different skill levels also receive positive feedback.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views in Planes?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Planes provide spectacular panoramic views due to the region's rugged mountainous landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18937727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frencantada Viewpoint – Toll de l’Estret loop from Planes&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent vistas. The Sierra de Mariola, with viewpoints like Buitreras, is also known for its expansive scenery. For truly iconic views, the medium-difficulty trail to the summit of Peñón de Ifach provides breathtaking coastal and inland panoramas.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting while hiking in Planes?
A: The natural areas around Planes offer chances to observe local wildlife. The Sierra de Mariola, for example, is a favored spot for birdwatchers, particularly due to the presence of griffon vulture nesting areas. Exploring the quieter trails and natural parks increases your chances of encountering various bird species and other local fauna.

Q: Are there long-distance hiking options available from Planes?
A: While many routes are day hikes, the extensive network of trails in the mountainous province allows for longer excursions. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10357824" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toll de l’Estret – Gorg del Salt de Planes loop from Planes&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longer moderate routes, covering nearly 13 km. For multi-day adventures, the interconnected trails within the Sierra de Mariola and Guadalest Valley offer opportunities to extend your journey and explore more deeply.
tips: The Barranc de L'Encantada, a spectacular route full of pools.
This pool is the most touristic since it can be reached by car and many families decide to spend the day there.
The Gorg del Salto de Planes, a small part of the Barranco de la Encantada, is a beautiful area for swimming.

From the Ermita del Cristo del Santo Cristo de Planes you can enjoy the best views of the entire region, it has a playground where you can distract the little ones in the house while enjoying the landscape, it also has picnic areas.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Barranc de l'Encantà Trail?
faqs.answer: The Barranc de l'Encantà Trail typically takes around 3.5 to 5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how many stops you make to enjoy the scenery and natural pools. The route itself is approximately 10.4 kilometers long.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The Barranc de l'Encantà Trail is generally considered easy to moderate. While much of the route follows accessible paths and rural asphalted tracks, the initial ascent from Planes towards the Hermitage of the Most Holy Christ of Planes is the most demanding part. Good fitness and sure-footedness are recommended for certain sections, especially as the path narrows within the ravine.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a varied terrain on this route. It starts with an ascent on a zigzagging footpath from Planes, then transitions to paths between olive and cherry tree terraces, dirt tracks, and some paved sections. As you delve deeper into the barranco, the path becomes narrower with high rock walls, featuring a mix of mountain hiking paths, roads, and unpaved trails.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Barranc de l'Encantà Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the charming village of Planes. You can find parking within the village itself. Some parts of the trail, especially near the main swimming areas like Gorg del Salt de Planes, are also accessible by car, offering options to start closer to specific points of interest.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several beautiful highlights. You'll pass by the medieval aqueduct and other historical structures in Planes. The climb to the Hermitage of the Most Holy Christ of Planes provides excellent views of the Beniarrés reservoir. Within the ravine, look out for the impressive Toll de l’Estret gorge, the tranquil Pool of the Barranco de l'Encantada, and the famous Gorg del Salt de Planes waterfall, a popular spot for wild swimming.

faqs.question: Is the Barranc de l'Encantà Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Barranc de l'Encantà Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, given the varied terrain and some narrower sections, it's advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially around other hikers and near any steep drops or water features. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with moderate hikes.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Barranc de l'Encantà Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically in spring, when the cherry trees are in blossom and the ravine is particularly verdant, often with impressive water flow after winter rains. Autumn is also a great choice for cooler temperatures and beautiful fall colors. Summer can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if hiking during this season.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Barranc de l'Encantà Trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for wild swimming along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The Barranc de l'Encantà is renowned for its natural pools and cascades, making it a popular spot for wild swimming. The most famous swimming hole is Gorg del Salt de Planes, characterized by a beautiful waterfall and a large stone wall. The water flow is most impressive after heavy rains, offering a refreshing dip on warmer days.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the primary route is the Barranc de l'Encantà trail itself, sections of this hike also follow parts of the 'El Calvari' and 'Revoltes dels Maulets' paths, adding to the diverse experience.

faqs.question: Is the Barranc de l'Encantà Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While parts of the trail are accessible and relatively easy, the overall difficulty is considered moderate due to the initial ascent and some potentially unmarked or narrower sections within the ravine. Beginners with a reasonable level of fitness and appropriate footwear should be able to manage it, but it's not a completely flat or paved walk.

waypoints.name: Pool of the Barranco de l'Encantada
Hermitage of the Most Holy Christ of Planes
